data.title: Extract spatial-layout JSON Schema to standalone schema file
data.description:
## What
Extract the JSON Schema definition from plugins/dso/skills/design-wireframe/docs/output-format-reference.md and write it to plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json as a standalone machine-readable schema file.

## Why
Done definition 4 for story 147a-5eec requires output validation against 'the existing schema'. No standalone schema file exists — only a fenced code block in output-format-reference.md. Unit test NM-11 and the figma-node-mapper.py implementation both need a concrete file to validate against.

## testing_mode: GREEN
No new behavior — extracting existing schema content into a dedicated file. No behavioral test required (schema content is not conditional logic).

## Files to Create
- plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json

## Implementation Steps
1. Read the JSON Schema fenced block from plugins/dso/skills/design-wireframe/docs/output-format-reference.md (the block starting with {'$schema': 'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'...})
2. Write it to plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json as valid JSON
3. Validate the file parses correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json exists
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json
- [ ] File is valid JSON
  Verify: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json'))"
- [ ] File contains required top-level fields from the schema (storyId, components)
  Verify: python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json')); assert 'storyId' in s.get('properties',{}) and 'components' in s.get('properties',{})"
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
